title: Self-Criticism as a Dominant Internal Pattern
description: Self-criticism often functions as an internal voice that constantly judges, compares, and finds fault. While it may have developed as a way to stay safe or perform well, it frequently leads to chronic stress, low self-worth, and emotional exhaustion.
observation_language: It seems like there’s a very harsh internal voice that’s often critical of your efforts and mistakes.
There appears to be a pattern of being much kinder to others than you are to yourself.
It looks like self-criticism may be showing up as a way to motivate yourself, even though it often leaves you feeling worse.
underlying_fears: Fear of not being good enough
Fear of being judged by others
